to take air into the lungs and let it out

very great or severe; far beyond normal
an unpleasant feeling caused by danger or threat

vocabulary.meaning: lucky; having good luck

vocabulary.meaning: to take place or occur
the measurement of something from end to end

vocabulary.meaning: an error or wrong action
to watch carefully or notice
a favorable time or chance to do something
a reward given for winning or achievement
a competition of speed; a group of people sharing origin

to say or do something as a reaction

sentences.en: The surprise party excited the whole family.

sentences.en: She faced her fear and spoke in front of the crowd.

sentences.en: We are fortunate to live in a peaceful country.

sentences.en: She trained hard every day to win the race.

sentences.en: He realized that hard work always pays off.

sentences.en: She responded to every question with confidence.

sentences.en: Starting a new business always involves some risk.

sentences.en: I often wonder why the sky is blue.

sentences.en: He has not given up yet and keeps trying.
sentences.bn: সে এখনো হাল ছাড়েনি এবং চেষ্টা করতে থাকে।

opts: from one side to the other
quiz.explanation: Across means moving from one side to the other or on the opposite side.

opts: to take air into lungs and release it
quiz.explanation: Breathe means to take air into the lungs and then let it out.
Characteristic means a typical or noticeable quality of a person or thing.

opts: to eat, drink or use up
quiz.explanation: Consume means to eat, drink, or use something completely.
Excite means to cause someone to feel very enthusiastic or eager.

opts: very great or far beyond normal
quiz.explanation: Extreme means very severe, intense, or far beyond what is usual.
Fear is an unpleasant emotion caused by the belief that someone or something is dangerous.
Fortunate means having good luck or being in a favorable situation.
Happen means to take place or occur, especially without being planned.
Length means the measurement of something from one end to the other.
Mistake means an action or judgment that is wrong or incorrect.
Observe means to watch something carefully and attentively.

opts: a favorable chance to do something
quiz.explanation: Opportunity means a set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something.

opts: a reward for winning or achievement
quiz.explanation: Prize means a reward given to the winner of a competition or for outstanding work.
Race means a competition in which people or animals compete to see who is fastest.
Realize means to become fully aware of something or to understand it clearly.

opts: to say or do something as a reaction
quiz.explanation: Respond means to say or do something as a reply or reaction to something.
Risk means the possibility that something bad or unpleasant will happen.

opts: to think with curiosity or feel amazed
quiz.explanation: Wonder means to feel curious and want to know something, or to feel amazement.

opts: up to this time or still
quiz.explanation: Yet means up to this time, still, or however — used to indicate something has not happened so far.

story.title: The Race Across the Mountain
story.body: Tariq had always wondered what it would feel like to win a race. Ever since he was a child, running had been his greatest characteristic — he was fast, focused, and fearless. But this year, he faced his most extreme challenge yet: the Mountain Cross Race, a competition that required runners to travel across a steep mountain trail of enormous length. The night before the race, Tariq could not sleep. Fear crept into his heart. He had observed other runners during training and realized that many of them were more experienced. He began to doubt himself and worry about the risks involved. His coach noticed and sat beside him. 'Breathe slowly,' she said. 'You are fortunate enough to be here. Not everyone gets this opportunity.' Her words excited something deep inside him. The next morning, the race began. Tariq moved across the rocky path with determination. Halfway through, he made a mistake — he took a wrong turn and lost precious time. For a moment, extreme panic set in. But instead of giving up, he responded quickly, corrected his path, and pushed forward. The crowd watching from across the valley began to cheer. Their energy consumed his fear and replaced it with strength. Step by step, breath by breath, he climbed. He did not yet know if he would win, but he realized that finishing the race was itself a prize. When he finally crossed the finish line, the crowd went wild. He had not come first, but he had won something greater — the courage to face his fear, take a risk, and respond to difficulty with strength. The judges observed his performance and awarded him a special prize for the most determined runner. Standing there, breathing hard, Tariq wondered no more about what winning felt like. He already knew.

comprehension.q: What mistake did Tariq make during the race?
What consumed Tariq's fear during the race?

opts: a special prize for most determined runner

comprehension.q: What did Tariq realize at the end?
opts: finishing with courage is itself a prize

listening.title: A Conversation Between Two Athletes
listening.script:
A: Are you nervous about the race tomorrow?
B: Yes, I have an extreme fear of losing.
A: I understand. But you have trained so hard. You should feel fortunate.
B: I realize that. But something bad could still happen.
A: Mistakes happen to everyone. The key is how you respond.
B: That is true. I observed the other runners today.
A: What characteristic do they have that worries you?
B: They consume less energy and breathe more steadily.
A: Then you must learn from that. It is a great opportunity.
B: I wonder if I can really win the prize.
A: Do not think about the prize yet. Think about running across that finish line.
B: You are right. I need to take the risk and give my best.
A: Exactly. The length of the race does not matter. Your attitude does.
B: Thank you. I feel excited now.
A: Good. Breathe deeply, stay calm, and remember why you started.
B: I will not give up. Not yet.
A: That is the spirit. You are going to do great.
B: Whatever happens, I will respond with my best effort.
A: That is all anyone can ask for. Good luck tomorrow.

comprehension.q: What is the athlete's extreme fear?
What characteristic of other runners worried the athlete?
opts: they consume less energy and breathe more steadily

comprehension.q: What does the friend say about mistakes?
opts: everyone makes them; the key is how you respond

comprehension.q: What does the friend say to focus on instead of the prize?

speaking.topic: Talking About a Challenge You Faced
speaking.prompt: Describe a time when you faced a fear or took a risk. Use words like fear, risk, realize, respond, opportunity, mistake, and fortunate.
speaking.example: Once I had to speak in front of my whole class. I had an extreme fear of speaking in public. But I realized it was a great opportunity to improve myself. I made a small mistake at the beginning, but I responded calmly and continued. After I finished, everyone clapped. I felt very fortunate and excited. That day I learned that taking a risk can lead to something wonderful. I still wonder how I managed to do it, but I am glad I did not give up yet.
tips: Start by describing the situation clearly
Use the word fear to explain how you felt
Say what risk you took and why
Explain the mistake you made and how you responded
Use fortunate and realize to reflect on what you learned
End with wonder or yet to show you are still growing

conversation.scenario: Two friends discussing life goals and overcoming challenges

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Do you have a big goal you are working toward yet?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Share your goal and how far you have come.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Have you ever made a mistake while chasing your goal?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Talk about the mistake and what you realized from it.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: How did you respond when things went wrong?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Explain how you responded and what helped you continue.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What characteristic do you think helped you the most?

steps.prompt: Name a characteristic and explain why it matters.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Do you feel fortunate to have the opportunities you have?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Reflect on your opportunities and what you wonder about the future.
